title: asterocephalus tataricus
intro: Asterocephalus tataricus, also known as Tatarian aster or Tatar aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone and phenolic compound content. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Asterocephalus tataricus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and may be used to support respiratory and gastrointestinal health.
